name: HMS Coverley (T 106)
events: 20 Jan 1944 At 1350 A/20, HMS Uproar (Lt. L.E. Herrick, DSC, RN) departed Naples for her 25th war patrol (20th in the Mediterranean). She was to perform a special operation. She was to act as a beacon during the Anzio landings (Operation Shingle).
She departed Naples in company with HMS Ultor (Lt. G.E. Hunt, DSC, RN). The submarines were escorted by HMS Coverley (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.R. Lord, RNVR).
The submarines parted company with the escort at 1730 A/20 and proceeded independently to carry out their patrol orders. (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
